doctor:
TARGET AUDIENCE: Doctors / Prescribing Physicians
- Focus on: mechanism of action, clinical rationale, pharmacological profile, drug interactions, prescribing considerations
- Use clinical terminology (they understand it) but keep it concise
- Emphasize: when to prescribe this vs alternatives, what makes this combination unique, safety profile
- Tone: peer-to-peer, evidence-based, concise — respect their time and intelligence
- Quiz questions: test clinical decision-making, not rote memorization
stockist:
TARGET AUDIENCE: Stockists / Distributors
- Focus on: product overview, key selling points, market positioning, demand indicators
- Avoid deep clinical details — keep it practical and business-oriented
- Emphasize: which doctors prescribe it, for what conditions, why there's demand, competitive landscape
- Tone: business-friendly, clear value proposition, confident
- Quiz questions: test product knowledge relevant to stocking decisions
retailer:
TARGET AUDIENCE: Retail Pharmacists / Chemists
- Focus on: what the drug treats, common customer questions, storage, dosage basics, when to refer to a doctor
- Use simple, practical language — avoid heavy clinical jargon
- Emphasize: how to counsel patients, what to tell them about side effects, storage instructions
- Tone: practical, helpful, patient-safety focused
- Quiz questions: test customer-facing knowledge
all:
TARGET AUDIENCE: Healthcare Professionals AND Retail Pharmacists/Vendors
- This video serves BOTH prescribers (doctors) and dispensers (pharmacists, retailers, vendors)
- Start with the clinical essentials (mechanism, indications, key pharmacology) but explain them in accessible language — no heavy jargon
- Then bridge to practical dispensing: patient counseling points, common questions customers ask, storage, and when to refer back to the doctor
- Tone: knowledgeable but approachable — like a senior pharmacist briefing both a new doctor and a new chemist at the same time
- Emphasize: what it does, why it works, who should take it, who should NOT, what to tell the patient, and practical tips
- Quiz questions: mix of clinical understanding (mechanism, contraindications) and practical dispensing knowledge (storage, patient advice)
- Keep medical terms but always follow with a plain-language explanation in the same sentence
